name: Faro del Curra, Cala Cortina, Sierra de la Fausilla y Parque de Batel — return from Cartagena
name_original: Faro del Curra, Cala Cortina, Sierra de la Fausilla y Parque de Batel — vuelta desde Cartagena
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cartagena offers diverse terrain, from flat coastal roads along the Caribbean Sea to rural landscapes with moderate inclines. The region features routes through regional parks and areas with hills, providing varied experiences for cyclists. Historic urban exploration within the Walled City allows riders to navigate colonial architecture and lively plazas. Coastal routes often provide views of the Mediterranean Sea and pass through natural features like salt flats. Best touring cycling routes around Cartagena The most popular touring cycling route is La Manga del Mar Menor — round trip from Playa de las Amoladeras, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes through salt flats. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Faro del Curra, Cala Cortina, Sierra de la Fausilla y Parque de Batel — return from Cartagena, a moderate 17.6 miles (28.3 km) path. It includes coastal sections and leads towards Cala Cortina, Cartagena's urban beach. Local touring cyclists also love the Tour of the Natural Park of Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán, a 23.0 miles (37.0 km) trail leading through a cliff mountain range, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Cartagena is defined by coastal paths, regional parks, and areas with hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat coastal rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Cartagena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cartagena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cartagena?
A: Cartagena offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 200 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 49 easy, 85 moderate, and 69 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Cartagena?
A: The terrain around Cartagena is quite diverse. You'll find flat coastal roads along the Caribbean Sea, rural landscapes with moderate inclines, and more challenging routes through regional parks and areas with significant hills. This variety allows for different cycling experiences, from leisurely rides to more strenuous climbs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy cycling routes in Cartagena?
A: Yes, Cartagena has several easy touring cycling routes suitable for families or beginners. There are 49 easy routes available. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, many coastal paths offer gentler terrain. For a moderate option that includes coastal sections and leads to an urban beach, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016718498/faro-del-curra-cala-cortina-sierra-de-la-fausilla-y-parque-de-batel-return-from-cartagena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro del Curra, Cala Cortina, Sierra de la Fausilla y Parque de Batel — return from Cartagena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic natural features or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views of the city and the Mediterranean Sea. You can cycle past natural features like the Marchamalo Salt Flats, which are part of routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016726337/lighthouse-cabo-de-palos-and-salinas-de-marchamalo-gravel-circular-through-the-regional-park-of-calblanque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lighthouse Cabo de Palos and Salinas de Marchamalo — gravel circular through the Regional Park of Calblanque&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes explore regional parks like Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas, and Peña del Águila, known for virgin coves and diverse ecosystems. You might also encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations&lt;/a&gt; on some tours.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Cartagena?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available for touring cyclists. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016710153/la-manga-del-mar-menor-round-trip-from-playa-de-las-amoladeras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Manga del Mar Menor — round trip from Playa de las Amoladeras&lt;/a&gt;, which offers coastal views and passes through salt flats. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016726337/lighthouse-cabo-de-palos-and-salinas-de-marchamalo-gravel-circular-through-the-regional-park-of-calblanque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lighthouse Cabo de Palos and Salinas de Marchamalo — gravel circular through the Regional Park of Calblanque&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a regional park.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cartagena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cartagena are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have explored the region using komoot, often praising the varied terrain, from coastal paths to challenging climbs in regional parks.

Q: Can I explore historical sites or urban areas by bike in Cartagena?
A: Absolutely. The historic Walled City of Cartagena provides a unique urban cycling experience, allowing riders to navigate through colonial architecture, lively plazas, and past colonial churches. The vibrant Getsemaní district, known for its street art, is also popular for exploration by bike. You can also visit landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3831954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cartagena Town Hall&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3455816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punic Wall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, for more experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Cartagena offers 69 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016737280/tour-of-the-natural-park-of-sierra-de-la-muela-cabo-tinoso-and-roldan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Natural Park of Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through a cliff mountain range and features significant climbs.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I spot while cycling in the region?
A: The regional parks and natural areas around Cartagena offer opportunities for wildlife spotting. The Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso, and Roldán Nature Reserve is a Special Protection Area for birds like the peregrine falcon and eagle-owl. Wetlands and mangroves in areas like La Boquilla are excellent for birdwatching, where you might see native and migratory birds, herons, crabs, and kingfishers. The Guillermo Piñeres Botanical Garden, a short drive from Cartagena, also hosts howler monkeys, iguanas, and toucans.

Q: What are some prominent viewpoints or peaks accessible by bike?
A: Cycling routes often provide breathtaking views from hills like Torres Park or Castillo de la Patos, and from lighthouses such as El Faro de Cabo Palos. For those seeking higher elevations, some routes may offer glimpses of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6581220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2939367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roldán Peak&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular for hiking and offer panoramic vistas.

Q: Is there mountain biking available near Cartagena?
A: Yes, for more adventurous cyclists, Tierra Bomba Island offers mountain bike tours with trails and hills. These tours combine sport with historical exploration of ancient fortifications and rides through green island vegetation, providing a different kind of cycling experience than traditional touring.
tips:
It is located on the Curra Dike, a structure that protects the port from the winds, helping ships avoid obstacles and find their way.
The Curra Dike Lighthouse is a tourist attraction in Málaga, especially for those visiting the port and the city.
It offers stunning views of the port and the city and is a popular spot for photography.

Four kilometers from Cartagena, Cala Cortina offers the peace and tranquility of the tranquil waters. A footpath leads directly to the beach and provides a pleasant walk with views of the natural harbor and the cliffs surrounding it. It is very popular, a favorite for many thanks to its calm, clear waters and there is also catering on the beach.

Cartagena has a 2000-year mining history, in the region around La Unión the remains from the 19th and 20th centuries can still be seen impressively. For fans of Lost Places and Mad Max / Lord of the Rings (-:

Rolling along the bike lane of the Batel Park we have beautiful views of the Castle of Cartagena or the Puerta del Socorro. An ideal place if we want to stop to rest and/or recover energy. The Batel Park has a large green esplanade where we can walk, ride a bike or play with the little ones.
